critic_raw: Re-examining the TFT vs LightGBM demand forecasting design. Three concerns: (1) The Tobit imputation model conditions on promotion status, day-of-week, trailing velocity, and inventory position - these are features also used in the main model. This does not constitute leakage since Tobit is a pre-processing step fit on training only. (2) The validation window (Jan-Mar 2024, 3 months) is relatively short for hyperparameter selection across 50 Optuna trials. This could lead to overfitting to validation, but both models share this limitation equally. (3) Cold-start exclusion is correct but the 90-day threshold is arbitrary - however this is a scoping choice, not a flaw. No fatal flaw identified.
defender_raw: All concerns are either design choices with clear justification or non-issues: (1) Tobit as pre-processing is standard practice; conditioning on overlapping features does not create circular reasoning. (2) Both models share identical validation window, so any limitation affects both equally and does not bias the comparison. (3) Cold-start threshold is a principled exclusion criterion that prevents contamination of the primary analysis. The design is sound.
adjudication_raw: Critic found no fatal flaw in second run. The Tobit imputation concern resolves to standard practice; the validation window concern affects both models equally. Verdict: defense_wins.
